rag_rules: Preserve the existing AADL structure and attach any generated annex only to the owning declared element with valid scope for that kind of content.
Keep component-type contract content separate from implementation-level content; do not move type-scope statements into an implementation unless the content is valid there.
Do not introduce references that cross scope boundaries or depend on subcomponent paths unless the target scope legally exposes them.
generic_rules: Maintain owner fidelity: insert new content into the intended owning component or implementation without changing unrelated declarations, features, connections, or properties.
Use minimal edits that preserve formatting and surrounding architecture; avoid restructuring the model when only annex insertion is needed.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE content is wrapped in an `annex agree {** ... **};` block.
Implementation-scoped content should appear only in valid implementation scopes and after any needed local structural declarations are already present.
anti_patterns: Do not place type-level port contracts in an implementation scope just because the current target is an implementation.
Do not use subcomponent port names or implementation-only paths from a scope where they are not visible.
Do not alter architecture elements or repair unrelated model issues while performing insertion.
irrelevant_topics: Stateful equation syntax and temporal recurrence details
Scalar helper type selection and expression typing rules
